blurb: Paul’s last charge rings like a church bell at dusk: preach the word, not trends; endure discomfort, not applause. In a season when voices compete for every ear, these verses re-center the pulpit and the private heart alike—God’s speech first, ours second, the world’s approval never.

verses.text: I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;
For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;
And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.
For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.
